reviews.review_text:
For less than the price of a pint you can be the owner of one of the most satisfying games ever made..

Everything is top notch in Vamp Survivors, the characters, the music, the world design and of course the gameplay; that was so fun that it kickstarted an entire genre. This game scratches an itch in your gamer brain like no-other. Cheers Poncle.

Playtime: 52.8 hours (on and off, and steadily climbing at time of review)

I’ve played this on and off since buying it and somehow ended up 50+ hours deep without ever really planning to.

Vampire Survivors isn’t really a game I “would finish” in the traditional sense. It’s more something I keep coming back to in bursts, and it slowly just eats time in the best way.

It’s simple, addictive, and very easy to lose yourself in. Runs always turn into “just one more go” moments, and before you know it, hours have gone past.

The value for money is insane. I bought it for £3.99 originally, and I always pick up the paid DLCs to support the devs because there’s just so much content packed in.

I’ve never really treated it like a game with an end, more like something I rotate in and out of depending on mood.

I love its secrets and plan on putting many more hours into it, trying to complete it as fully as I can.
if you don't have a life (like me) and love to grind for hours just to unlock new characters and stuff, this game is for you.

The most barebones roguelike experience, without any flare or depth to make it worth your money or your time. The visuals are underwhelming (both the pixel art and the HUD) and the gameplay loop, the central aspect of any roguelike game, is pretty repetitive and gets boring after a couple of runs. Would not recommend it.

While the gameplay is relatively simple it is very addictive. The music is great, the gameplay loop is engaging, the graphics are charming. There is so much content, characters, upgrades, secrets, weapons, levels, etc.
It can get a little old after 200 characters, but each character comes with new weapons and/or relics.
Rating: 1000 Monsters out of 0 Vampires

This game is inexpensive, starts simple, becomes surprisingly complex, and is incredibly addictive. Better yet, the developers are awesome human beings, and seem to truly love their fans. They are what gaming companies should be, people who do what they do because they love it!
It's completely bizarre how many hundreds of hours of satisfying bullet heaven gameplay you get for only €4,99. There is SO much to discover and SO much to do in this game.
genuinely the first time i've ever regretted 100%ing a game. dopamine stimulus nexus and not much else
Totally worth the price. Multiplayer is fun. Gameloop is fun. And my brain is turned off. Love it!

This is criminally under-priced, I feel like I've been scamming the devs. Great game, fun blast to play when you got a bit of spare time here and there. A lot of things to unlock, and there is a sort of sense of beating the game of which I've only just achieved after these hours.
there needs to be a pc accessory that can blink for you because i forget to do so when i'm playing this game

This game was my first 'bullet hell' game and now I am obsessed.

The game starts quite slow, but as you play more, unlock more, and power up more, the game gets faster and more intense.

There is a lot of content to unlock and I get the impression there is a lot more for me to unlock.

This is also a great game to let your mind go numb, just sit back and hit the keys and watch the action.
Really fun, easy to get into and a lot of replayablility.
Cheap, addictive, polished, still gets updates after the release of the newest game, and it even runs on grandma's laptop.

Pros: 
- It is unbelievably cheap for the sheer quantity of content you get
- It is a very fun game
Cons:
- I have been informed that the obscene number of particle effects may give some people migraines

If you had asked me to rate this game at any time until literally 2 days ago, I would've said it's mid. But I just didn't have ANYTHING. Once I got to level 2 and unlocked POE, it got so much more fun. Fantastic game, I have the Amogus DLC (because I'm immature) and I'm considering downloading the Ante Chamber (Balatro) DLC. If you don't want to buy it, download Survivor.io on your phone and play it, if you like it, BUY THIS GAME!!! Survivor.io ripped this game off, and is basically an inferior and mobile version of this. PLEASE BUY V.S.!!!
This is one of the most addicting games I've ever played in my life. It's visually a lot sometimes, but if you don't mind that, this is one of the coolest games I've come across!

Highly- highly - addictive gameplay loop. The time-based runs make this great for quick play sessions. Unsure if there's actually a lot of mechanical gameplay depth but the sound effects, weapon animations, and visuals make it all worth it.

It's cheap. It's also easy to play. I find it to be a game you can play readily when you're bored without getting too involved. It's also not a huge time crunch, since you can easily stop whenever. I like discovering the combinations to upgrade weapons. And the RNG appeals to me in a gacha-type of way.

Fun grindy rouge like with lots of content and various strategies
absolutely worth the price tag
Great game! Sounds like microwave popcorn when enemies die! Opening chests seems like I won a fantastic prize on a tv gameshow. Great timekiller.
Отличная игра для вечера после работы. Когда нет сил или желания погружаться во что-то сложное, Vampire Survivors подходит идеально. Простая в освоении, затягивающая и отлично помогает расслабиться.
Slow to start but it picks up and is really fun.

Love this game sooo much! Extremely fun and addicting when you get in the groove of things... One critique I have, is that when you open a chest and the whole music and dramatic reveal happens, it takes a few seconds too long of a wait in between when you can click the exit button from the chest. Hope that made sense, lol; not the worst thing, but it does halt the pace a bit! Besides that, I love it!!!!

A nice, cheap game that's super easy to lose yourself in! There's enough variety in the characters, weapons and levels that you can easily keep going for hours. A good one to sink some hours into if you like achievement hunting too! I'm about 50 hours in now and still feel like I have plenty left to do! Well worth the small price for the amount of replayability.
Vampire Survivors is Great, Easy / Challenging all around Fun, Pretty Cheap too even with the DLC.

Te hace click como te pasaba cuando eras pibe...  malo como el paco, rico como la merluza del Dark and Darker. Cada nivel que pasas es un raquetazo de sabor que te deja preguntandote que sentido tiene el mundo real...
At first it seems simplistic but as time goes on the game reveals a vast amount of things to do and unlock. Hightly addictive and highly recommended.
Many hours of fun, some of the achievements felt a bit annoying but nothing that soured the experience for me, it's great.

You can spend DAYS on this game and still be playing it to unlock different items/weapons/characters to play.  Worth every dollar spent.  Co-op mode with friends makes it a lot more fun and glad people playing from their phone can crossplay cause a couple of my friends didn't want to repurchase the game.

This is NOT my typical game type of choice. I typically find these sort of games growing boring very quickly, repetitive, and have a relatively low skill ceiling. BUT that is not the case for Vampire Survivors. Once I started playing I couldn't stop. It's addicting in the best "one more time" way. So many characters to choose from and unlock, so many different weapons to upgrade. Every run is different. It's hard and there is a relatively steep learning curve but it's worth it. It's basically endless, you fight hordes of enemies (zombies, bats, monsters, etc) of varying difficulty and unlock different skills (whip, holy water, knives, magic, etc). Play to your preference. And there are so many different combinations. Dozens of characters to unlock, too, each with their own gameplay caveat. There is a lot to see, and unlock, and even if you're not into this sort of game I think you'd be surprised just how addicting it is!
fun game, at start runs can be long, but soon unlock stuff and it becomes faster and fun
Super addictive, easy to learn and really fun to play! I love roguelike/lite games and this is in my top 10!

So fun and intense. Never mind the crappy-looking title screen, like it's straight from an HTML game. The game is simple and gripping. In fact, it's so intense and fun I have to force myself to take breaks to sort of rest my brain. And so cheap too! Awesome.
Unfortunately it is 2am and I have to work tomorrow morning. This game is too damn addictive. Recommend.

Figure I might as well review it, since I just got all steam achievements.

At time of writing, I've only dabbled in the crossover DLC, since my main priority was getting all steam achievements. I have also logged, at time of writing, Just over a hundred hours in. I am less than halfway done. That's counting me doing ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, too. The game is a behemoth, and an absolute blast to play. 

The game is far more enjoyable to play than it is to watch. You'll start off weak and slowly gain the power and skill to absolutely bend the game's levels over your back, and then you get to try really ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. With the right combination and support, even the worst weapon can be made into a valuable part of your arsenal. Or you can unleash your full power with all the overpowered Darkana and give your computer and yourself a seizure!

Game's good. And dead cheap. If you aren't epileptic, give it a shot. If you are epileptic, this game will kill you.
very fun game, bit slow at the start but gets so much better as you go, so much content base game
Great game expecially for the people who likes games focuses on progress

Vampire Survivors is one of those games that's simple to learn but incredibly hard to put down. The gameplay is fun, the progression feels rewarding, and every run keeps me coming back for "just one more try." Definitely one of the best Roguelike games I've played. 

I recommend this to fans of roguelike games:)

Downloaded this game to test steam on an old PC that I installed Linux on. Was the only game that I thought it could handle without a GPU. First impression was that I would never play this after the initial test because the controls were too basic and I couldn't imagine it being fun. I've since logged 69hrs of game play, and it's the best $5 I've ever spent. 10 out of 10. Addictive and fun.

This hides the fact that it is an unapologetic grindfest rather well with a simple but effective gameplay loop of avoid enemies, pick up powerups. It leaves me wanting to experiment with new characters and in general play more than it does frustrate me so yeah, I think it's worth a play.

No comment on any of the DLC - I have not tried any of them yet. The base game is expansive enough that it gave me more than enough to chew on as of time of reviewing.

Found [spoiler] Dracula [/spoiler] and the sequence after made yell out, curse it out at the middle bit for a niche reference, and then cry once it was all said and done. 

As a castlevania fan, and a vampire survivors fan, this silly 5$ game stole over 200 hours from me (Yes I know steam says less, I have it on steam, switch, and other platforms, thanks cross save) and will steal a bunch more. I feel like we've forgotten that some games can just be fun. Sometimes repetition can be comforting, and after a day of work brain needs to go off rather than getting worked up and yelling at ranked. If the game looks remotely interesting to you, pick it up and just sit back, relax, and watch the pretty colors, if you're a castlevania fan, spend the extra 4 bucks and watch a team give the series you love and grew up with one hell of a love letter.

I wanted to play Vampire Crawlers.
I purchased the bundle and decided to give Survivors a shot.
I haven't played Crawlers yet.
The simplicity of the gameplay, in conjunction with the vast upgrade possibilities, as well as the eventual insanity that it unfolds right before your eyes... yeah, it's pretty fun!
Boredom killer, extremely addictive and fun.

If I was stranded on an island and I could only choose ONE game to play for the rest of my life, it would hands-down be Vampire Survivors. No hesitation. This game has ruined me in the best possible way. It might be the most addicting experience I’ve had since Azur Lane, and that’s saying something.

From the moment you boot it up, Vampire Survivors looks deceptively simple. You control a character who auto-fires weapons in every direction while hordes of monsters swarm you. Survive for 30 minutes, level up, pick new weapons or upgrades, and try not to die. That’s the loop. Sounds basic, right? Wrong. This game is pure, concentrated dopamine.

A fantastic game that's nostalgic, but also fresh. This game takes me back to simpler times of finding secrets and chasing unlockable characters. It's engaging enough to make you want to play one more round, but at times simple enough that you can just turn your brain off and chill. Between all the RPGs, MMOs, and FPSs this is a breath of fresh air to play something that's just different. It respects your time in a time where few games do that and it respects your wallet with a great cost to playtime value. Bonus points for no micro transactions or endless DLCs like some of my other favorites.
Mindlessly and brainlessly fun. Cool way to spend some time while listening to audiobook or music.
amazing game solo and wth friends, i hope they keep adding updates and expansions!!!!!!

I got this right around when the game first came out. I got two hours in, was unimpressed, uninstalled it, and only noticed it every so often when browsing my library. I don't know what sparked reinstallation, but I realized I put the game down only moments away from realizing how amazing this game actually was.

The two weeks prior to writing this review, this game has absolutely consumed me. I found so many secrets and little steps to take to continue unlocking new means of play that after 100 hours, I STILL have to make goals and find crazy new barriers to break. I'm not finished exploring this game and what it has to offer.

This is one of those games I will look back on wishing to experience it my first (second?) time all over again.

If you can stomach the first two hours of a slog, you're pretty much set. 

I don't want to go into too much detail to the mechanics, but at its base, it's an action rogue-like, sort of reverse bullet hell against an unrelenting, never ending horde. As you play, you gain constantly gain access to a wealth of knowledge as to what your aims should be. You snowball in opportunities.

Once, I did get confused as to what my next hurdle could be, so I decided to take an opportunity to go to the internet and look up what was next. I made an explicit effort to avoid doing so for most of my experience, and as it turns out, what I needed to do next was so glaringly obvious in hindsight that I had to kick myself. I would suggest an avoidance to spoiling the mysterious progression, and if I had to give only two pieces of advice, I'd say to pay close attention to the unusual, and that everything you need to know for your next step is granted to you by the game.
Simple game that gets very complex. Great puzzles for unlocking new weapons and characters
Runs on on my Spin 714 great. Fun game if you want to kill time. Some achievements i had to look up since im bad and couldnt figure em out.
YESSS!!! The videos DO NOT do the game justice!! It is so much more fun to play than it is to watch- and for the price I don't think you can beat it. Also I love vampires!!!
One of my favorite games, amazing how much content there is and how replayable it continues to be.
one of the best rougelikes i've played in a while, even when you get all achievements there's still unlocks to obtain
10/10 would survive again. Runs great everywhere. Amazing fun and couch coop

This is a laid back and relaxing game (if you let it be) that can also be played with friends.  Plus, it is great for achievement hunters because there are so many to work on obtaining!  Do not forget that the music (soundtrack) is amazing!
Majorly fun time waster. Complex and fast moving.
It has a lot of depth to the game and is really addictive and fun to play

Se existe um jogo que entende perfeitamente o significado de “só mais uma partida”, é Vampire Survivors.

A proposta dele é extremamente simples: sobreviver a hordas intermináveis de criaturas enquanto seu personagem evolui automaticamente através de armas, upgrades e combinações absurdamente satisfatórias. E talvez seja justamente essa simplicidade que torne o jogo tão viciante.

A gameplay funciona de uma forma quase hipnótica. Você controla apenas a movimentação do personagem enquanto os ataques acontecem automaticamente, e conforme os minutos passam, a tela vai se transformando em um verdadeiro caos visual cheio de projéteis, efeitos, monstros e números explodindo por todos os lados. E sinceramente? É maravilhoso.

O mais impressionante é como o jogo consegue transformar algo tão simples em algo absurdamente divertido. Cada partida parece diferente graças à enorme variedade de personagens, armas, builds e evoluções. Descobrir combinações novas e perceber que você virou uma máquina imparável de destruição nunca perde a graça.

E existe um detalhe que torna tudo ainda melhor: colocar uma música de fundo enquanto joga. Tem algo extremamente satisfatório em simplesmente desligar a mente, ouvir suas músicas favoritas e sair amassando hordas infinitas de mobs durante 30 minutos sem perceber o tempo passar. O jogo entra quase num estado de “flow” absurdo.

Além disso, Vampire Survivors entrega muito mais conteúdo do que parece à primeira vista. São dezenas de personagens, mapas secretos, relíquias, modos extras, desafios e desbloqueios que fazem você sempre ter algum objetivo novo para correr atrás.

Visualmente ele é simples, mas funciona perfeitamente para a proposta. A estética retrô combinada com o caos crescente na tela cria uma identidade própria muito marcante. E a trilha sonora também ajuda bastante na atmosfera frenética do jogo.

No fim, esse é um daqueles jogos que provam que você não precisa de gráficos ultrarrealistas nem mecânicas complexas para criar algo extremamente divertido. É barato, viciante, satisfatório e perigosamente difícil de largar depois que você começa.

English

If there’s one game that truly understands the meaning of “just one more run,” it’s Vampire Survivors.

The concept is incredibly simple: survive endless waves of creatures while your character automatically attacks and evolves through weapons, upgrades, and ridiculously satisfying combinations. And honestly, that simplicity is probably what makes the game so addictive.

The gameplay feels almost hypnotic. You only control your character’s movement while attacks happen automatically, and as the run progresses, the screen slowly turns into complete chaos filled with projectiles, explosions, enemies, flashing effects, and damage numbers everywhere. Somehow, it never stops being satisfying.

What’s most impressive is how the game turns such a simple formula into something insanely fun. Every run feels different thanks to the huge variety of characters, weapons, evolutions, and builds. Discovering overpowered combinations and realizing you’ve become an unstoppable destruction machine is always rewarding.

And there’s one thing that makes the experience even better: playing your own music in the background while you play. There’s something incredibly relaxing and satisfying about turning your brain off, listening to your favorite songs, and absolutely destroying endless hordes of mobs for 30 minutes without even noticing time passing.

On top of that, Vampire Survivors has way more content than it initially seems to have. There are tons of characters, secret stages, relics, unlockables, challenges, and extra modes that constantly give you new goals to chase.

Visually, the game is simple, but it fits the experience perfectly. The retro-style visuals combined with the escalating screen chaos create a very unique identity. The soundtrack also does a great job supporting the frantic atmosphere.

In the end, this is one of those games that proves you don’t need ultra-realistic graphics or complicated mechanics to create something incredibly fun. It’s cheap, addictive, satisfying, and dangerously hard to stop playing once it hooks you in.
I had heard this game was addicting but damn. Every time I feel like I'm near the bottom of what it has to offer, it reveals a whole other layer of things to unlock and discover.

Very addictive.
Easy gameplay.
Neat progression.
The deepest insanity you will endure, I do not understand this game and will continue to not understand this game.
This game prodded Steam create an entire game category/tag for it called "Bullet Heaven".  If that isn't a testimonial to a game, I don't know what else is.

Very fun and addictive bullet hell-style game. Runs fantastic on Steam Deck and has become one of my main go-to games when travelling or away from home. It’s one of those games where you can pick it up for a quick 20-30 minute session, have an absolute blast, then put it back down again without feeling like you need to commit hours to it.

There’s a ridiculous amount of unlocks, builds, characters and combinations to mess around with, and the “just one more run” feeling is very real. Even when you fail a run, you usually unlock something new or make progress towards the next upgrade, so it always feels rewarding. So it's got that roguelike progression to keep you coming back. 

Despite the simple graphics, the gameplay gets completely chaotic in the best way possible once the screen starts filling with enemies, effects and projectiles. Surprisingly satisfying and way more addictive than it first looks. Perfect game for chilling on the sofa, travelling, or killing time.

This may be in the Top 20 Greatest Video Games Ever Made and I am NOT kidding. I just sorta picked it up, and it just turned into the gift that kept on giving. The way it just keeps exponentiating its own content, and playing its cards close to the chest less and less to reveal over time what a sprawling web of stuff to do and see it is. An hour in, I thought I might play it for seven. Seven hours in, I thought I might play it for thirty. Thirty hours in, I hoped I would never find a reason to stop. It's so full of love, especially with all its expansions. Pick this thing up, I'm begging you.
Great game for mindless fun and not even thinking about playing while I actually am playing.
this game is really nice u can make alot of builds and once u get into hyper mode it just feels ways better i would recommend it. its only 5 euro

Simply put: Vampire Survivors is incredibly addictive. The pixel-art graphics are great, and the constant motivation to upgrade or combine weapons in the next run keeps the gameplay exciting for hours.

Finding new characters and hidden items on the maps makes progression even more rewarding. On top of that, the DLCs constantly add fresh content and give you even more reasons to keep playing.

It’s one of those games where you always think: “Just one more run…” 😅

I would definitely recommend it.

There is no way I cannot recommend this game. Just Ode to Castlevania alone is worth a play, it comes with just 50 more hours of gameplay attached.
For its price it offers so much and now that Warhammer survivors is coming I am expecting great things.

The replayability of this game is fantastic, each character has their own special ability which makes them all have the possibility of being OP. Look forward to unlocking more characters and getting all of the achievements. As of typing, Imelda and Gennaro are my favorite ones to do runs with :D

Buy it. even if not on sale. i've never really enjoyed achievement hunting but it's really fun for this game. (128/243 as of review time) AND, you get the most content ever for such a low price. i'm 70% through completing the base game collection and halfway through the castlevania dlc and i've already spent almost 50 hours. there's soooo much more content so value per cent is abysmally high. it will keep you blissfully engaged for as long as your brain allows for it when you are dopamine maxxing - which is IMO forever :P 

if on sale, buy it don't even think about it. and also check out vampire crawlers.
Fun and addicting but only for so long.
The most fun I've had with a game in a long time. Gives all that nostalgia that you're longing for from the NES days.
Way too much content for such a cheap price, game is simple but fun
Just simple fun. Works well on steamdeck and games have a time limit which is excellent

Vampire Survivors is one of those games that looks like it should not work.

Tiny little characters.
Pixel monsters.
Weapons firing everywhere.
A screen full of nonsense.
A title that confidently mentions vampires while mostly refusing to provide any.

And yet, somehow, it is absolute magic.

I mainly played this one co-op on a mate’s PC, which is probably the correct way to discover it. Sitting there as two grown adults, staring at a screen full of bats, skeletons, flashing lights, garlic, holy books, knives, explosions and poor life choices, pretending that any of it involved skill rather than just surviving the consequences of our own upgrade decisions.

It was brilliant.

At first, you think, “Right, this is simple.” You move around. Your weapons fire automatically. Enemies wander toward you with the confidence of people who have not yet met evolved garlic. You collect gems. You level up. You choose another weird upgrade.

Then, without warning, the game becomes a full-screen pokies machine of violence.

Numbers everywhere.
Treasure chests.
Music kicking off.
Weapons evolving.
Your character turning into a walking workplace hazard.
Both players yelling advice that is neither helpful nor calm.

That is the loop.

It is cheap, chaotic, addictive, and somehow far deeper than it has any right to be. There are builds, synergies, secrets, unlocks, characters, weapons, and enough flashing nonsense to make you feel like you are either mastering the system or being gently hypnotised by an Italian slot machine with skeletons.

Two-player makes it even better. Not because it becomes tactical, exactly. More because you can share the blame. Someone picks the wrong upgrade. Someone drags the mob the wrong way. Someone says, “We’re fine,” exactly three seconds before things become very much not fine.

And then you start again.

I don’t know how a game this simple managed to become this satisfying, but it did. It is one of those rare games where “just one more run” is not a statement. It is a medical condition.

Vampire Survivors is ridiculous, generous, addictive, and an absolute cracker with a mate.

Still not sure where the vampires are.

You have been warned.
It's honestly one of the best rouglites that is out there. It's really fun and has so much replayablity

It's so peak, by the middle of most runs I have no idea what's even happening on screen anymore
Co-op is super fun as well
Great game. Its perfect for Steam Deck. If you like Vampire Survivors you will also enjoy Vampire Crawlers.

I absolutely LOVE this game, all of the different secrets and combinations are amazing! I find myself frustrated with certain boss challenges but that makes it all the more rewarding when you finally get it. Highly recommend this game

I 100%'d Vampire Crawlers and so it gave me the itch to come back and do the same for Vampire Survivors. I had a great time doing so. I really like the design space this game sent so many other indie games on. If you like games like this and are looking for other great entries in the genre, I recommend Vital Shell or Megabonk.

Some good ol' fashioned arcadey power fantasy nonsense, and I love it! Underneath it all though, there's a surprisingly deep game with so many secrets-abilities, characters, levels, bosses, and more- I'm sure there are still dozens I haven't found.

Also read the entries in the Beastiary, they're hilarious
finally got every achievement. good game the boss approves
A TON of fun and very addicting. Plays similar to another game called Megabonk but FAR more options and WAY more fun! Plus the ability to play with others instead of just solo is nice as well

I don't think you need me to tell you how good this game is. It spawned an entire genre of rip-offs and clones.

You could probably put quad-digit hours into this game. I don't know how close I'll get to that, but I would not be surprised to see that on anyone's account.

This game is really fun. The graphics turned me off at first because I’m not really a fan of how it looks, but it really doesn’t matter. It’s a lot of fun to walk around and spew death, and some of the combos you get are insanely fun and clever.

Loads of carnage and destruction and easily fits into my time-poor shedule!.. 
Great Game for those whose a looking to blow stuff up and yet still be challenging!

This game has an interesting difficulty curve.  Its balanced right at the start, then you quickly slaughter everything easily until it finally catches up when you hit the hardest parts and need to worry about putting a build together (barely).  Only the bottom screen frost guy you have to kill is any kind of challenge sometimes.

Its very fun though, its like an endless slotmachine you can only win paying out a 30 minute jackpot.  Suprisingly satisfying.  Reminds me of a lategame diablo build slaughtering lvl 1 stuff.

Its a great monetization model.  Pay once get infinite dopamine hits that would usually be purchased lootboxes.  Find a magnetorb in the game and gems pour into your character.  This game stomps the satisfaction of being a p2w whale as your character explodes with gems, treasure and power for 30 mins straight.
Survive the digital drug dealing vampires.

I HAVE NO WORDS. i have played this godforsaken game for hundreds of hours on mobile and playstation, getting all unlocks and secrets both times. ALL. OF. IT. TWICE

and im gonna do it a third time lmao
poncle is a mad genius. support your local italian, buy VS.

Vampire Survivors beautifully illustrates how a small scope, 2D, single player indie game can achieve a profound level of interactive engagement through minimalist input. By boiling the core mechanic down entirely to avatar movement while weapons attack automatically, the game creates an incredibly tight reciprocity loop where every micro adjustment of the arrow keys directly alters the behavior of massive enemy hordes. Weaving through hundreds of sprites to herd enemies and collect experience gems feels deeply responsive, yielding a hyper rewarding feedback loop of screen clearing damage numbers and tactile audio cues. Ultimately, the game proves that meaningful interactivity doesn't require complex button layouts; by limiting physical actions to pure positioning, it expertly shifts the player's focus toward strategic navigation and pure engagement.

At first I didn't think much of this game. Low end graphics, you only move the character around. However, once I played a couple of runs, the dopamine hits and the with it comes the addiction. This is my 4th version of the game I own (PS4, PS5, Epic) and if I had another system, I would also get it on there.

I mean, it's 2026, and it's Vampire Survivors.

This game has about as much brand recognition as whatever you ate for dinner.  The game costs very little, has an insanely addictive gameplay loop, and a staggeringly enormous amount of content.  The DLC are cheap as well, and each one adds a ton of extra content.

I'd say this should be in every Gamer's library.  There will be a time where you need something simple, with lots of action and dopamine spewing goodness.  There are many games like this, but none ever quite deliver what this game does.

If you are on the fence, I recommend it.  If you see it on a sale discount, you should absolutely get it.
Couldnt stop playing it, very addicting and very fun 10/10 wont sleep again
The game is legit fun and it's awesome seeing so many Castlevania characters, but don't get your hopes up too high in a game with rng when your luck stinks like mine. >u>'
Bloody farkin awesome game, HIGHLY RECOMMEND!!! Sunk 60hrs already in 6 days xD
This game and halls of torment are super fun and addictive games.

[h1]The game that pioneered a whole new subgenre of roguelites but in 2026, there's no denying there's better ones out there[/h1]

I feel like this could have had a bigger impact if I hadn't played other survivor-likes before but honestly, it's pretty boring. I still recommend it as it had a HUGE impact on the sub-genre but it lacks a lot of stuff other survivor-likes brought afterwards. 

The "in-the-moment decision making" isn't really there. It's not really a "skills" game but a "knowledge" game. If your build isn't good enough, you'll lose and there's no amount of "being good at playing the game" that'll help. Most runs end up feeling similar-ish where at the 15-20mins mark you basically become a stationary bullet hell yourself and sit there and watch the screen blow-up. It's fun the first couple times but it gets boring quick.

Vampire survivors is one of the only games I've bought on full discount, It's a very diverse game that has really good gameplay. It has good content without DLC's. And speaking of DLC's. The DLC's are very fun for games you are familiar with. And having Collabs with popular games such as, Among us, Balatro, and much more. It also has fun upgrades. Combo's to do with weapons. And maps that are fun in there own ways. Not to mention the good pricing for only 5 dollars. For under 10 dollars. You could get this game + a DLC of your choice. And under 20 dollars. You could receive all DLC's including the game itself. Costing 16.73 for the entire thing. Another upside to this game is that the multiplayer is super fun, if friends of your have this game, It's really fun to play while having friends on your team. They could assist during runs and have OP combo's when you play. I would REALLY recommend to play with friends as it is really fun and provides more strategy when playing with friends.

Overall :
If your looking for a game that is cheap, well-rounded, and a fun Singleplayer - Multiplayer Game, this game is what your looking for!

Fun and casual horde survivor game. Several hidden gems throughout the stages to find and unlock. Choosing how to build your weapons loadout can really change how each playthrough goes. Leveling up the weapons also has some hidden gems. Different types of weapons for different player styles are available. Easy to get lost for several hours running through the stages.
One of the few Survivors games with click to move, great for accessibility thanks to the dev!!
